problem text: Compute the indefinite integral of x log(x) with respect to x.
problem solution: We seek $$\int x\,\log(x)\,dx$$. We proceed by integration by parts, letting $$u=\log(x)\implies du=\frac{1}{x}\,dx$$ and $$dv=x\,dx\implies v=\frac{x^2}{2}$$. By the integration by parts formula $$\int u\,dv = uv - \int v\,du$$, we have $$\int x\,\log(x)\,dx = \frac{x^2}{2}\,\log(x) - \int\frac{x^2}{2}\cdot\frac{1}{x}\,dx = \frac{x^2}{2}\,\log(x) - \frac{1}{2}\int x\,dx = \frac{x^2}{2}\,\log(x) - \frac{x^2}{4} + C$$, where $$C$$ is the constant of integration. Hence, the solution is $$\frac{x^2}{2}\log(x) - \frac{x^2}{4} + C$$.
problem hint: Use integration by parts by designating the logarithm function as u.
